title: Where did hard work equals worth come from?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for tracing the hard-work belief back to its source.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Week 5 of the MONEY program. Six prompts for tracing where the hard-work belief came from, what it has cost, and what a different rule might look like.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Week 5 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you're unsure where the rule that money requires struggle actually came from.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Week 5 of the MONEY program. The hard-work belief is often inherited rather than chosen. These prompts trace it back.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
journal prompts as a practice
first.
The practice
Warm-up
What does earning feel like in my body right now?
Write without editing for two minutes. Notice where the feeling sits.
What's the rule I've been running about effort and money?
Write the rule as plainly as you can. "Money requires..."
Closer in
Who taught me that hard work is how money comes in?
Write the person or the scene. How old were you?
What has the hard-work rule cost me?
Write the specific cost: time, rest, ideas you never tried, projects you dismissed as too easy.
Have I ever earned money in a way that felt light or surprising?
Write the specific example. What did you do with that knowledge?
What would I try this month if I believed earning could be easier?
Write the specific idea. No editing for feasibility yet.
Where this practice comes from
Adapted from the Week 5 reflection prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
